_sources.discrepancies: NCSL lists OK as 'photo ID requested' (non-strict). SoS confirms voters without photo ID may execute a personal affidavit and vote a regular ballot. Consistent.

earlyVoting.notes: Early voting available Thursday through Saturday before Election Day. Hours vary by county. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting for runoff. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting available Thursday through Saturday before Election Day. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting is available Thursday through Saturday before Election Day at county election board offices.

acceptedIds: Oklahoma driver's license or ID card
Voter registration card (photo on card issued after 2012)
votingRules.expirationRule: Photo ID must not be expired. Voter registration card with photo is acceptable if it was issued after 2012. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
votingRules.impedimentDeclaration: Voters without acceptable photo ID may sign an affidavit and cast a regular (non-provisional) ballot.
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Oklahoma prohibits use of mobile phones and electronic devices in the voting booth.
additionalRules: Semi-closed primary: registered Republicans and Democrats may only vote in their own party's primary. Independents (no party) may vote in either party's primary but not both.
Runoff rule: if no candidate receives a majority in the primary, the top two advance to a runoff. A voter who voted in one party's primary may only vote in that party's runoff.

eligibility: Will be absent from the county on Election Day and early voting period
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: Must be received by the county election board 7 days before the election
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the county election board by 7 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Absentee ballot must arrive at the county election board by 7 p.m. on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.

runoffRules.ruleExplanation: Oklahoma rule: if you voted in one party's primary, you can only vote in that same party's runoff. If you did not vote in the primary, you may choose either party's runoff.
